problem: What triggers cancer cells to leave primary tumor and colonize new tissue?
mechanism: mRNA therapy to upregulate/downregulate gene expression
hypothesis: What triggers cancer cells to leave primary tumor and colonize new tissue? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of mRNA. mRNA therapy to upregulate/downregulate gene expression directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: In vitro cell culture assay with 8 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The art of science is asking the right question, not answering. — Max Planck paraphrase
